summary: Chromium credential-dumping toolkit (cookies + saved passwords, App-Bound Encryption bypass)
details: The package ships a Chromium infostealer CLI for Windows. It locates Chrome/Brave/Edge installs, copies the Cookies and Login Data SQLite databases from each browser profile, extracts the DPAPI master key from Local State, defeats Chrome v20 App-Bound Encryption by launching the browser under a debugger to recover the in-process AES key, and writes decrypted cookies (Netscape format) and saved passwords (plaintext) to./<browser>_cookies/ and./<browser>_passwords/ in the working directory via a native addon (addon.decryptCookie / addon.decryptPassword). A license gate fetches a key allow-list from raw.githubusercontent.com/tabo1337/SaatPCBKod/refs/heads/main/key.txt (a mutable personal branch), caches it in ~/.baksen_cache, and gates execution on a sha256 hash of BAKSEN_LICENSE_KEY salted with 'baksen_secure_salt_2024'. Publisher metadata is placeholder/unattributable: repository/bugs/homepage point to github.com/yourusername/baksen, description is 'hadiyapic', author is 'ibrahim1337'. The package's sole documented function is browser credential extraction; output is local with no built-in remote exfiltration, but the toolkit is a turnkey infostealer and the mutable author-controlled allow-list lets the publisher gate which installers can run it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
